mood.label: RISK-OFF
mood.summary: Geopolitical tensions in the Middle East are spiking oil prices and driving a flight from risk. Tech and growth are selling off, with a clear rotation into energy and defensive sectors.

indicesNarrative: US futures are sharply lower across the board as escalating US-Iran tensions send oil prices surging over 5%. The VIX is spiking and Treasury yields are rising, signaling a classic risk-off move as investors flee equities for safety and hedge against inflation risks.
technicalsNarrative: Technical data not available. Defer to price action and risk-off macro narrative.

sectorsThemes.name: Energy
sectorsThemes.note: Led the market yesterday on surging oil prices.

sectorsThemes.name: Technology
sectorsThemes.note: Worst performing sector; hit by rotation out of growth.

sectorsThemes.name: Health Care
sectorsThemes.note: Gained ground as investors moved into defensive names.

sectorsNarrative: A clear defensive rotation was evident in the previous session. Capital fled Technology (-2.39%) and Industrials, flowing directly into Energy (+2.84%) and classic defensive sectors like Health Care and Utilities.
industryNarrative: Semiconductors led the downside, dragging the tech sector lower after Samsung's earnings failed to ignite further buying despite strong results. Oil and gas exploration and production companies were the primary beneficiaries of the geopolitical risk premium being priced into crude.

movers.why: Upgraded to Outperform by Evercore ISI amid surging oil prices.
Upgraded by multiple analysts including Raymond James and Goldman Sachs, citing conservative outlook and stabilizing consumer perception.
Fell after Wolfspeed filed a patent-infringement lawsuit against the company.
Downgraded to Equal Weight at Morgan Stanley, which slashed the price target to $53 from $79.
Downgraded to Market Perform from Outperform at Keefe Bruyette.

watchlist.note: Watching for breakdown amid broad tech weakness and semi sell-off.
High-beta name vulnerable in a risk-off tape.
Mega-cap tech under pressure, will act as a market tell.
Observe for signs of institutional selling in core holdings.
Consumer discretionary exposure could be a headwind.
Ad-spending sensitivity in a slowing growth environment.
Another key mega-cap to watch for signs of distribution.
Part of the weak semiconductor group.
High-momentum AI name, susceptible to sharp pullbacks.
High-valuation software could see multiple compression.
Bitcoin proxy, watch for correlation with risk assets.

traderLens.name: Minervini
traderLens.view: Market is in a correction. No new long positions should be initiated. Focus on capital preservation.

traderLens.name: Ted Zhang
traderLens.view: Clear rotation into Energy (XLE) and out of Tech (XLK). Follow the institutional flow.

traderLens.name: Clement Ang
traderLens.view: This is a C/D-grade environment. Risk is high, setups are failing. Best to sit on hands.

traderLens.name: SRxTrades
traderLens.view: Volatility is expanding. Wait for volatility to contract and clean pivots to form before considering new entries.

traderLens.name: Jeff
traderLens.view: Respect hard stops. The market character has changed, do not fight the tape.

traderLens.name: Qullamaggie
traderLens.view: No momentum for breakouts. The environment is hostile to breakout strategies. Stay cash.

traderLens.name: Composite
traderLens.view: PASS on new long entries. The geopolitical catalyst has shifted the market to risk-off. Monitor energy names for relative strength, but the primary goal is capital preservation. Wait for the tape to stabilize.

alert: RISK-OFF: Geopolitical tensions spike oil, futures sharply lower.

news.headline: US stock futures tumble as Trump says Iran ceasefire is 'over'; oil climbs
Wall Street slipped: S&P 500 down ~0.4%, Nasdaq off ~1.2%, dragged by semiconductor and AI-related tech weakness
FOMC Minutes in focus for clues on inflation and growth assessment
Occidental Petroleum Upgraded to Outperform at Evercore ISI
